content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886
August 21, 2006


John R. Harper, City Attorney
City of Murrieta
Harper & Burns LLP
453 S. Glassell Street
Orange, California 92866

RE: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. W-06-161


Dear Mr. Harper:

This letter is in response to your request for informal assistance, on behalf of the City of Murrieta Councilmember Warnie Enochs, regarding the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ The Act requires the Commission to provide written advice, including informal assistance, to any person whose duties under the Act are in question or to that person's authorized representative. (Section 83114(b); regulation 18329(e)(1).) However, we may decline to provide informal assistance if it would be inappropriate or otherwise not in the public interest to do so. (Regulation 18329(c)(4).)

Based on facts provided in a telephone conversation on August 17, 2006, we decline to provide informal assistance to you at this time. Your current question involves an analysis of a series of interrelated decisions, some of which have already been made. Issuing informal assistance at this time would relate to past conduct and, therefore, would not further the impartial, effective administration of the Act so mandated by section 83111.

Therefore, your request is withdrawn. I enclose a copy of regulation 18329 for your review.



¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

File No. 06-161
Page No. 2


If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

John Wallace
Acting General Counsel

[signature]

By: Brian G. Lau
Counsel, Legal Division

Enclosure

The request is withdrawn because issuing informal assistance would relate to past conduct and would not further the impartial, effective administration of the Act as mandated by section 83111.

sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Declination/withdrawal letter with clear conclusion section. No formal QUESTION section header present but question can be synthesized from the RE line and body text. Document explicitly states 'your request is withdrawn.'

The request is withdrawn because issuing informal assistance would relate to past conduct and would not further the impartial, effective administration of the Act as mandated by section 83111.
